recording_notes: MONITOR RECORDING — phone filming RCA monitor from CLOSE (best RCA framing in the series). Same lab as V21/V23. Task area fills most of the frame. 1920x1080 at 30fps. Dark/black suture — good visibility. Moderate suture debris on perforated board at left (less than V23). Blue/teal laparoscopic instruments visible on desk below monitor. Dust marks on monitor screen. STORZ-type white perforated base above foam block.

frame_analyses.description: Drain visible centered on pink foam block. No instruments. Moderate debris at left on board. Best RCA monitor framing in the series — close, task area fills frame.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean start. Best monitor recording quality from this lab.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments entering from top. Dark suture clearly visible extending downward from instrument tip. TIMING STARTS ~5s.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Quick entry. Black suture well-visible against white background.

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments at drain. Needle driving in progress. Curved needle and suture loop visible above drain. Left instrument stabilizing, right driving needle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Active needle pass at 24s. Still in placement.

frame_analyses.description: Suture placed. Both dark suture ends visible from drain. Instruments repositioning for first throw. Suture extending to both sides from drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement complete ~30s (24s from first instrument). Transitioning to first throw.

frame_analyses.description: First throw wrapping. Both instruments at drain with suture loops. Drain being manipulated. Dark suture wrapping visible.
Second throw in progress. Both instruments above drain pulling suture high. Large dark suture loops visible extending upward. Instruments spread wide.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw complete ~52s (~22s). Second throw wrapping/tightening.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw tightening. One instrument from lower left pulling laterally. Purple/dark suture line extending horizontally. Drain compressed at center — hourglass shape from knot tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw complete ~68s (~16s). Third throw being cinched. Good lateral tightening technique.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting phase. One instrument from upper right at drain. Dark suture tails extending from knot. Knot visible with wrapping at drain center. Cutting underway.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw complete ~86-88s (~20s). Cutting started ~88s. 12+ seconds into cutting.

frame_analyses.description: Still cutting. Instrument from left with scissors at drain. Purple/blue suture tail extending horizontally to the right. Long tail still present. Knot at center.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 42+ seconds into cutting. Long tail visible — cutting slower than V24.

frame_analyses.description: Still cutting at 150s. Large instrument from upper left at drain. Suture wrapping visible around drain. Long tail extending downward. Drain being handled for final cuts.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 62+ seconds of cutting. REGRESSION from V24's 40s cutting. Back to V21-V23 range.

frame_analyses.description: Near completion. One instrument still at upper area with dark suture loop. Knot visible at drain center — dark X/star pattern with moderate-length tails. Drain slit appears mostly closed but possible small gap visible. Drain slightly off-center — knot may be pulling it to one side. Drain not avulsed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completing at ~155s. Video ends at 159.8s. Tails moderate length — not as short as V22/V24's clean cuts. Possible small gap is concerning.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw 30-52s (22s). Surgeon's knot assumed. Good visibility with dark suture. Confidence: moderate.
Third throw 68-88s (20s). Good lateral tightening visible at 80s — hourglass drain compression. Confidence: moderate.

suture_placement.placement_notes: Dark suture through drain visible at 32s. Placement area appears correct. Marks not independently visible. Conservative 2mm estimate.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: At 158s, drain slit shows possible small gap — the knot appears to be pulling the drain slightly to one side rather than centering it perfectly. The drain is not avulsed but the closure quality may be slightly inferior to V22/V24. Moderate-length tails. Confidence: moderate due to better framing.

technique_summary: V25 at 150 seconds represents a REGRESSION from V24's breakthrough 117s, returning to the V22-V23 plateau of ~150-155s. The most significant regression is in cutting: 67 seconds versus V24's 40s — a 68% increase. This confirms that V24's cutting breakthrough has NOT yet consolidated into consistent performance. Placement at 22s and throws at 58s total are consistent with recent performances and remain solid. The cutting regression is the entire story of V25 — without it, the trainee would be in the 120s range. The better camera framing (best RCA recording in the series) provides moderate confidence (0.45) and reveals a possible small gap in the drain closure that would not have been detectable in V23/V24's lower-quality recordings. If V25 followed V24 in the same session, fatigue explains the cutting regression. If a different session, the V24 cutting improvement requires further consolidation.
improvement_suggestions: CUTTING REGRESSED: 67s vs V24's 40s. The breakthrough has NOT consolidated. Continue dedicated cutting drills.
The cutting regression alone accounts for 27 of the 33 seconds slower than V24 — fixing cutting is still the single highest-ROI improvement.
Possible small gap in drain closure — focus on centering the knot directly over the slit rather than pulling to one side during tightening.
EXCELLENT camera positioning — this is the best RCA monitor recording in the series. Maintain this framing for all future recordings.
Placement and throws are STABLE and consistent — these phases are approaching mastery.
If same session as V24: the fatigue pattern is mild (150s vs 117s = 33s, compared to V19-V20's 86s crash). Better fatigue management.
If different session: V24's cutting technique needs deliberate reinforcement before it becomes automatic.
strengths: BEST RCA MONITOR FRAMING in the series — close, task area fills frame, dark suture visible
Placement at 22s — consistently excellent across V21-V25
Drain not avulsed — 25 consecutive videos without avulsion
If post-V24 in same session: only 33s fatigue regression vs V18-V20's catastrophic 86s+ crashes

phase_comparison.placement_22s: Consistent. V24: 18s. V22: 20s. V23: 24s. Stable excellent.
phase_comparison.throws_58s: Consistent. V24: 56s. V22: 70s. V23: 66s. Slight slowing from V24.
phase_comparison.cutting_67s: REGRESSION. V24: 40s. V22: 57s. V23: 58s. Back to pre-breakthrough level.

key_observations: 150s / 442 FLS — regression from V24's 117s/477 back to V22-V23 plateau.
CUTTING REGRESSION: 67s vs V24's 40s. V24's breakthrough NOT consolidated.
Cutting alone accounts for 27 of 33 seconds slower than V24.
Placement 22s + Throws 58s = 80s non-cutting time. V24: 74s. Only 6s difference — non-cutting phases stable.
BEST RCA recording quality — close framing, dark suture, 0.45 confidence.
Possible small gap detected — first time flagged with this confidence level. May have been present in prior videos but undetectable.
If same session as V24: mild fatigue (33s vs historic 86s+ crashes). Progress on fatigue management.
If different session: V24's cutting speed needs reinforcement to become consistent.
V22-V25 average excluding V24: 152s. V24 remains an outlier until replicated.
25th video: the trainee has a stable 150s plateau with occasional peaks (V14: 107, V24: 117).
